channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish president warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: National auto parts retailer closing all California stores
item.description: Advance Auto Parts plans to close all remaining locations in California by mid-March as the automotive giant undergoes a massive restructuring plan.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: Pickleball isn't named after a dog: How the sport really earned its moniker
item.description: You may have heard a rumor about the game being named after a dog. Spoiler alert: That isn't the case.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: 12 people wounded by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers, police say
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Venezuelan parents watch from home as their son is laid to rest in Texas after drowning
item.description: EAGLE PASS, Texas (AP) — The cries of Liliana Olivero watching from Venezuela through a cellphone pierced through the somber Christian music and gusts of wind as her son's casket was lowered into the ground.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: An Associated Press journalist has served as a media eyewitness to the execution of Brad Sigmon by firing squad Friday in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A car pulled from a river may tell what happened to an Oregon family of 5 that went missing in 1958
item.description: After two days of dredging, a crane on Friday pulled from the Columbia River a Ford station wagon that officials believe belonged to an Oregon family who disappeared while on a trip 66 years ago to find Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Carolina man executed by firing squad is first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: A South Carolina man convicted of murder was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner to die by that method in 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — From heartbreak and devastation to outrage, Emily Pike's tragic death is stirring heavy emotions and spotlighting a crisis that has long plagued Native American communities, where a disproportionate number of people have been killed or have gone missing.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss was sentenced Friday to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump expected to sign order to modify Public Service Loan Forgiveness program
item.description: Public service employees who are part of a federal loan forgiveness program could be at risk of having to pay back their student loans if their work doesn't align with the president's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Fed chair Powell gives remarks on state of economy
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will give an update on the state of the economy Friday afternoon — less than two months since President Trump returned to the Oval Office. The remarks, part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump suggests he may not change daylight saving time
item.description: President Donald Trump indicated Thursday he may not push to end daylight saving time as Americans get ready to spring forward this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WGN 9

item.title: Monarch butterflies wintering in Mexico rebound this year
item.description: The number of monarch butterflies wintering in the mountains west of Mexico City rebounded this year, doubling the area they covered in 2024 despite the stresses of climate change and habitat loss, experts said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KRON4

item.title: 12 indicted migrants allegedly assaulted officers at South Texas ICE lockup
item.description: A federal grand jury in South Texas has indicted 10 Mexican nationals and two men from Guatemala on charges of assaulting two correctional officers, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei announced.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: War heroes, a bomber and military firsts among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: Photos seemed to be flagged for removal simply because their file included the word ”gay,” including an image of the B-29 aircraft Enola Gay, which dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walgreens set to go private in deal worth nearly $10 billion
item.description: Walgreens Boots Alliance says it has agreed to be acquired by private equity firm Sycamore Partners as the struggling retailer looks to turn itself around after years of losing money.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian politician challenges Donald Trump Jr. to charity boxing match
item.description: Canadian Sen. Patrick Brazeau challenged Donald Trump Jr. to a charity boxing match over the “bogus” tariffs implemented this week by his father, President Trump. "I know, I still can't believe it myself.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: White House mocks Democrats' over ‘choose your fighter' video
item.description: The White House has joined the online roasting of Democratic House members for their viral “choose your fighter” TikTok video. “Democrats not be cringe challenge. Level: impossible,” the Trump administration’s rapid response account posted on the social platform X along with the video.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KTLA 5

item.title: List of Social Security Administration offices DOGE is closing
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency has started to make large budget cuts, resulting in the closure of multiple SSA offices.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis hits 3-week mark of hospitalization
item.description: Francis offered a first public sign of just how weak he is on Thursday by recording an audio message that was broadcast to the faithful in St. Peter’s Square.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: A court-appointed lawyer has advised a federal judge to let the Justice Department drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams but prevent prosecutors from ever reviving the case so it doesn't hang over him “like the proverbial Sword of Damocles.”.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Job cuts last month higher than any February since 2009
item.description: U.S.-based employers cut more jobs last month than in any February in 16 years, according to a new report from outplacement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's plans to slash IRS staff could derail agency amid tax season
item.description: It would likely contribute to a longer-term trend at the agency of diminished audit rates, minimal tax enforcement for the most sophisticated taxpayers, and the use of outdated technologies that’s been going on for decades.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WGN 9

item.title: At least 3 skiers buried in Alaska avalanche, officials say
item.description: An avalanche in the Alaska backcountry swept away and buried three skiers who were believed to be dead under a pile of snow nearly as deep as a 10-story building, Alaska State Troopers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: By MARK THIESSEN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
